---
title: "TRAC Asylum Scraper"
author: "Joe Yerardi / The Philadelphia Inquirer"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true # Export a single, self-contained HTML file when rendered
    theme: flatly
    toc: true # Add table of contents
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: hide # Hide code by default but allow toggling
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load required packages
library(httr2) # For making web requests to TRAC's server
library(jsonlite) # For translating the TRAC API's JSON response into R
library(tidyverse) # For tidy data processing, cleaning and manipulation

# Build the base URL
trac_url <- paste0("https://", "tracreports.org/phptools/immigration/asylum/graph.php")

# All codes (e.g. court, representation, outcome) can be found in the "network" tab of your browser's "developer tools" window
# Example API call: https://tracreports.org/phptools/immigration/asylum/graph.php?stat=count&timescale=fymon&base_city=30&represented=1&denial2=2&timeunit=number
# In this example, the statistic is "count" (numbers vs. percentages), the court code is "30" (Philadelphia), the representation code is "1" (not represented) and the outcome code is "2" (granted). By changing these codes, we can get data for different courts, representation statuses and outcomes.

# Store the TRAC ID for English
english_id <- "16" 

# Define a list of the courts we want to scrape. 
# The name on the left becomes our label; the value on the right is sent to TRAC's server
court_targets <- list(
  "Philadelphia" = "30",
  "United States" = "All"
)

# Define the four outcome codes TRAC uses for case decisions
outcomes <- list("total" = "All", "granted" = "2", "denied" = "1", "other" = "3")

# Define the specific demographic codes we'll pass to the server (i.e. none, representation, language, custody)
target_lists <- list(
  "none" = list("total_cases" = "All"),                               
  "represented" = list("represented" = "2", "not_represented" = "1"), 
  "language" = list("total_lang" = "All", "english" = english_id),         
  "custody" = list("never_detained" = "1", "released" = "2",          
                   "detained" = "3", "not_known" = "4")
)
```

```{r API call function, include=FALSE}
# This function communicates with TRAC's server
fetch_trac_data <- function(court_code, denial_code, param_name = NULL, param_value = NULL) {
  
  # Start building the request with the base parameters every query needs
  req <- request(trac_url) %>%
    req_url_query(
      stat = "count",        # We want raw numbers, not percentages
      timescale = "fymon",   # We want the x-axis to be Year-Month
      base_city = court_code,# Insert the court ID (e.g., "30" or "All")
      denial2 = denial_code, # Insert the outcome code (e.g., "All", "1", "2")
      timeunit = "number"    # Again, ensuring we get counts
    )

  # Check if we are passing a specific demographic parameter (like custody or language)
  if (!is.null(param_name) && param_name != "none") { # If param_name is NOT "none"...
    req <- req %>% req_url_query(!!param_name := param_value) # ...add that parameter to the URL
  }
  
  # Execute the request
  resp <- req %>% 
    req_retry(max_tries = 3) %>% # Retry up to three times and if it fails...
    req_user_agent("Mozilla/5.0") %>% # disguise as a web browser...
    req_perform() # and perform it

  # Read the server's text response, parse the JSON and pull out just the "timeline"" list
  raw_df <- fromJSON(resp_body_string(resp))$timeline
  
  # Safety check: if TRAC returns nothing (e.g. no English cases that month), return an empty tibble
  if (is.null(raw_df) || length(raw_df) == 0) return(tibble(fymon = character(), count = numeric()))
  
  # Convert the list to a data frame
  as.data.frame(raw_df) %>%
    select(fymon, count = number) %>%
    # Remove commas from the count and convert to numeric
    mutate(count = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", as.character(count))))
}
```

```{r looper function, include=FALSE}
# This function loops through all the options for a specific dimension
scrape_dimension <- function(court_name, court_id, param_name, targets) {
  cat(sprintf("\nScraping %s dimension: %s\n", court_name, toupper(param_name)))
  
  # Loop through the 'targets' list (e.g., represented vs. not_represented) and bind the results into a single data frame
  map_dfr(names(targets), function(target_label) {
    target_value <- targets[[target_label]]
    cat(sprintf(" -> Fetching: %s\n", target_label))
    
    # Loop through the four outcomes (i.e. total, granted, denied, other)
    map_dfr(names(outcomes), function(outcome_name) {
      denial_code <- outcomes[[outcome_name]]
      
      # Call our API function with the specified variables
      df <- fetch_trac_data(court_id, denial_code, param_name, target_value)
      
      # If we get data back, save it to the data frame
      if(nrow(df) > 0) {
        df <- df %>% mutate(
          court = court_name, 
          dimension = param_name,
          category = target_label, 
          outcome = outcome_name
        )
      }
      # Pause for 0.3 seconds before the next loop so we don't crash TRAC's server with too many requests at once
      Sys.sleep(0.3) 
      return(df)
    })
  })
}
```

```{r execution, include=FALSE}
# Loop through our list of courts (e.g. Philadelphia) and, within each court, map over the specified dimension (e.g. language), feeding everything into our looper function
all_data <- map_dfr(names(court_targets), function(court_name) {
  court_id <- court_targets[[court_name]]
  map_dfr(names(target_lists), ~scrape_dimension(court_name, court_id, .x, target_lists[[.x]]))
})
```

```{r formatting, include=FALSE}
# Split and format into four individual data frames, one for each dimension (e.g. totals)
cat("\nFormatting into separate data frames...\n")

# Totals
totals <- all_data %>%
  filter(dimension == "none") %>% # Grab only the totals data
  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = count, values_fill = 0) %>%
  select(court, fymon, total, granted, denied, other) %>%
  arrange(court, fymon)

# Representation
representation <- all_data %>%
  filter(dimension == "represented") %>% # Grab only the representation data
  rename(representation = category) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = count, values_fill = 0) %>%
  select(court, representation, fymon, total, granted, denied, other) %>%
  arrange(court, representation, fymon)

# Custody
custody <- all_data %>%
  filter(dimension == "custody") %>% # Grab only the custody data
  rename(custody_status = category) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = count, values_fill = 0) %>%
  select(court, custody_status, fymon, total, granted, denied, other) %>%
  arrange(court, custody_status, fymon)

# Language
language <- all_data %>%
  filter(dimension == "language") %>% # Grab only the language data
  unite("col_name", category, outcome, sep = "_") %>% # Combine the "english" vs. "total_lang" category with the "granted"/"denied"/"other" outcome to get column names like "english_granted"
  pivot_wider(names_from = col_name, values_from = count, values_fill = 0)

# Create a list of expected column names
expected_cols <- c("total_lang_total", "total_lang_granted", "total_lang_denied", "total_lang_other",
                   "english_total", "english_granted", "english_denied", "english_other")

# If a column (e.g. english_granted) is missing because there were zero cases, manually create it and fill it with zeros so our code doesn't crash
for(col in expected_cols) { if(!col %in% names(language)) language[[col]] <- 0 }

# Subtract the English totals from the overall totals to get the non-English totals for each outcome 
language <- language %>%
  mutate(
    # Note the hyphen ("non-english" instead of "non_english") so the upcoming pivot_longer split works correctly in the next step
    `non-english_total`   = total_lang_total - english_total,
    `non-english_granted` = total_lang_granted - english_granted,
    `non-english_denied`  = total_lang_denied - english_denied,
    `non-english_other`   = total_lang_other - english_other
  ) %>% 
  rename(
    total_total = total_lang_total, total_granted = total_lang_granted, 
    total_denied = total_lang_denied, total_other = total_lang_other
  ) %>%
  # Fold columns back down into long format
  # names_sep = "_" splits "english_granted" back into two distinct variables
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(court, dimension, fymon), names_to = c("language", "outcome"), names_sep = "_") %>%
  # Pivot outcomes horizontally again to match our standard formatting
  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = value, values_fill = 0) %>%
  # Change the hyphen back to an underscore
  mutate(language = str_replace(language, "-", "_")) %>%
  select(court, language, fymon, total, granted, denied, other) %>%
  arrange(court, factor(language, levels = c("total", "english", "non_english")), fymon)
```

```{r export, include=FALSE}
# Export the data frames as CSVs
totals %>% write_csv("data/exported/totals.csv")
representation %>% write_csv("data/exported/representation.csv")
language %>% write_csv("data/exported/language.csv")
custody %>% write_csv("data/exported/custody.csv")

cat("\nSuccessfully exported all four files to data/exported/\n")
```
